definition: Unwholesome thinking shaped by hostility or ill will in the MN 19 twofold division.
notes: Use this record in ethical-thinking contexts such as MN 19. The compound prefers ill-will wording rather than the headword default aversive distraction because the passage is sorting kinds of hostile thinking, not naming the hindrance as a full practice category.

example_phrases.notes: Second member of the unwholesome class in MN 19.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use for MN 19-style ethical analysis of hostile or ill-willed thinking.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not inherit aversive distraction mechanically from the headword when the point is the hostile content of thinking rather than the hindrance framework as a whole.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the compound from collapsing into generic anger-language or into the broader hindrance label.
